intro: Teads and Dable overlap on scores and pricing posture, so the decision comes down to which problem each was actually built for. Teads is outstream video ad marketplace for premium publishers. Dable is native content-recommendation ad network, strong in Asia. Pick Teads when the job is content publishers monetizing mobile web video with brand-safe premium demand and you accept high lock-in; pick Dable when it is content publishers with significant kr, jp, or southeast asian audience and you accept high lock-in. The data we have shows Teads at opaque pricing and product information with average developer experience, and Dable at opaque pricing and product information with average developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Teads is a poor fit for mobile game developers seeking performance-driven rewarded or interstitial fill, and Dable is a poor fit for western publishers or apps needing video, rewarded, or banner formats. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Teads is for outstream video ad marketplace; Dable is for native content-recommendation ad network; decide based on whether you can accept sales-call pricing.
verdict_a: Choose Teads if you can absorb a custom-quote sales cycle and the work maps to outstream video ad marketplace for premium publishers.
verdict_b: Choose Dable if deep integration is an acceptable price for the depth it buys and the work lines up with native content-recommendation ad network, strong in Asia.

faq.a: Neither Teads nor Dable publishes entry pricing that lets us compare directly — Teads uses a custom model and Dable uses a custom model. Get quotes from both before assuming one is cheaper.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Teads to Dable?
faq.a: Moving from Teads to Dable is feasible, but Dable is rated high lock-in — expect your integrations and workflows to need rework to match its model. Budget for a parallel-run period rather than a hard cutover.

Both Teads and Dable rate the same on developer experience (3/5). The decision on DX then comes down to taste — which CLI, UI, or workflow matches your team's habits. A short side-by-side trial is the quickest way to tell.

faq.q: Is Dable a good alternative to Teads?
faq.a: Yes — Dable is a reasonable alternative to Teads for content publishers monetizing mobile web video with brand-safe premium demand.. The practical differences are custom-vs-custom billing and high-vs-high lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Dable as a credible swap.
